key_insight: A transfer-learning plus hybrid Cross-Entropy–Lovász optimization makes BiSeNetV2 much more robust to reflective seams without adding model size or speed cost.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical lightweight perception upgrade for real-time robotic welding in construction, where harsh lighting and metal reflections often break seam detection.
method: Fine-tune a BiSeNetV2 backbone with transfer learning for seam segmentation.
Use a hybrid Cross-Entropy–Lovász loss to stabilize learning and improve boundary quality.
Keep the architecture unchanged to preserve FLOPs, parameter count, and inference speed.
evidence_signal: The method reached 81.76% Joint IoU and 90.73% mIoU, improving Joint IoU by 22.36 points over the OHEM baseline and recovering 96.33% of severe zero-IoU failures under reflective conditions.
watch_item: Results are compelling for reflective welding scenes, but deployment still depends on domain match and the availability of representative seam images from the target construction environment.
paper_title: Enhanced Seam Segmentation for Automated Welding Robot in Construction Through Transfer Learning: Addressing Limitations of Bilateral Segmentation Network
